  • Industry: Technology Hardware, Storage and Peripherals

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 10/23/2025): 32 out of 540

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 10/23/2025): 4 out of 28

  • Market Capitalization: $29.22B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

Pure Storage is a dominant player in enterprise storage, specializing in ultra-fast flash technology. This speed, combined with software that works across company-owned data centers and cloud platforms, allows businesses to run demanding applications like AI and data analytics efficiently. Data center operators also offer Pure Storage architecture and services to their tenants.

Source Link: Pure Storage Investor Presentation

The company's Q2 FY2026 revenue grew 13% Y/Y supported by major hyperscale partnerships, including a strategic deal with Meta (META). Recurring subscription revenue reached $415M, which represents 48% of total revenue and demonstrates robust demand for flash-based, cloud-optimized storage solutions.

PSTG showcases excellent profitability, including $802 million in cash from operations, which is 579% above the sector median. This reliable financial cushion allows Pure Storage the flexibility to weather potential downturns and fund future growth, strengthening its competitive position.

PSTG Profitability Grade

While PSTG’s valuation appears stretched, the company maintains a 0.7x PEG ratio that is a 58% discount to the sector, indicating the stock may be undervalued relative to its earnings growth potential. In addition to extraordinary momentum, PSTG has earned a universal endorsement from Wall Street analysts with 19 FY1 up revisions vs. zero downward in the last 90 days. This show of confidence from investors and analysts, coupled with excellent fundamentals, makes PSTG a great data center software play.

3. Johnson Controls International plc (JCI)

  • Sector: Industrials

  • Industry: Building Products

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 10/23/2025): 36 out of 619

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 10/23/2025): 2 out of 39

  • Market Capitalization: $71.03B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

Johnson Controls supplies critical products like chillers, building automation systems, and intelligent controls to help safeguard data centers and maintain uptime. Data centers now account for about 10% of company sales and are growing rapidly, supported by strong demand from both hyperscalers and colocation customers. In Q3 2025, adjusted EPS grew 11%, fueled by double-digit order growth in the segment. These gains are reflected in the company’s stellar profitability grade, supported by a TTM EBITDA and Levered Free Cash Flow Margin that are 20% and nearly 74% above the sector median, respectively.

JCI Profitability Grade

The company boasts a record backlog of $14.6B and improved free cash flow, suggesting solid forward growth potential. JCI showcases a FWD EPS Long-Term Growth (3-5Y CAGR) that's 37% above the sector median, while FWD ROE growth stands at 11.85% vs. -1.42% for industrials. While recent momentum has slowed for the stock, analysts remain optimistic about the company’s earnings potential, with 19 FY1 Up Revisions versus zero downward in the same period.

JCI Revisions Grade

JCI provides investors with targeted exposure to the rapidly expanding data center maintenance sector, backed by strong fundamentals and a healthy project backlog.

Concluding Summary

The AI boom has transformed data centers into critical infrastructure, with the market growing at 25.6% annually and AI expected to drive 70% of data center demand by 2030. Beyond widely-discussed chips and utilities, many data-center-specific stocks remain overlooked.

SA Quant has identified three top Quant-ranked stocks well positioned to capitalize on data center growth. Applied Digital Corporation, Pure Storage and Johnson Controls offer a mix of strong profitability, value, growth, momentum, and earnings revisions for investors looking for exposure to the data center ecosystem.

We have many stocks with strong buy recommendations, and you can filter them using stock screens to suit your specific investment objectives. Consider using Seeking Alpha's 'Ratings Screener' tool to help find stocks that achieve diversification into desired sectors you like. Alternatively, if you're looking for a select number of Quant Strong Buy recommendations on a monthly basis, you might want to explore Alpha Picks.
